Any suggestions 🤔?

author: @aquahoodjd
text:
Robert your awesome 👍 what about t-shirts and a windmill or  breakaway view of different gears ? XL-T? I'm 2m tall and a big V with orangutan arms (a natural swimmer). I love your *school* as it is and I've learned so much from you. You are a wonderful teacher. My grandmother was a career educator and her husband, my Grandfather, was a metal machinist for the railroad post WWII. He let her go get her Master in Education following the war and she was the only woman in she University, the closet to her home in Appalachia (Eastern Kentucky). She was likely the first woman with a Master in Eastern Kentucky. She made her father teach her how to drive at 14, she was born in 1914 ( 1928 she was driving all the time and she continued into her late 80s and died at 99.5. She walked with a walker and never stopped reading even when it took a large light and magnifying glass. She was and is my hero. I was s late bloomer. ADHD and when I turned 20 or so went from a write off to an overachiever - BA in Philosophy - Honor's Society in Philosophy, Juris Doctor, Master in Public Health (I was the only non-M.D. or .min Master in Nursing or PhDs but still was Dean's List, peer reviewed journal published, UN published, and now use Free CAD, Blender, 3D printing, maker, hobbyist Electrical Engineer, Data Scientist (this I was s Research Assistant and a Assistant Attorney General doing the damages for KY but the National Association of Attorney Generals took my methodology and applied to all 50 states as nobody had done such a granular level piece of health economics and damages to the state treasury by a single pharmaceutical companies by dumping one synthetic opioid in the . market and I was able to show a legally defensible chain to every single executive branch from Oxycontin. They used my work in the recent NAAG vs Perdue Pharma and nobody challenged my damages calculation. It was a huge yet focused pull of data the deputy tried to shut down but the AG said "Give it too him and when the Uni Dept of Biostatistics saw my work they asked who did this? I said I did but I'm only a novice scientist and the Director said, "Son, your no novice!" They said how could you have possibly done this? I just came off a Yale University NIH Grant in Malaysia studying the effects of opioids and was given SAS, SPSS, NVIVO and R. I had to sske the IT department load R into my computer. None of the lawyers had a qlue what I was up to but the Uni did as did the NAAG when they heard about it. I didn't know until I called back and one of the new lawyers there said oh you're the guy I said what do you mean the guy he said the guy who did the Purdue Pharma damages calculation! I said yes I did and he told me the story of what happened afterwards. That deputy tried to shut me down because for him that was his little baby and he thought I was trying to outdo him when really I was hired to do that job! I know the attorney general and he knew where I just come from and so that's what I was hired to do. I did not work left and went back to my normal job which is international business law. I also have a maker and I went into law because I was manipulated by my father and I found an aptitude test from when I was 12 said I should have been a scientist or a doctor and that's why I went back for my MPH. It really blows because I never really liked being a lawyer that much but because I'm pretty sharp I guess they tell me I could be good at whatever I was doing so long as I was interested by it and I would have preferred to be an engineer or a doctor and would do that if I had the time to do it over. 

I'm going to see about joining a university to teach law because I've had experience globally an an unusually high amount at a really high level so I most assuredly have the experience in the CV but I want my PhD in philosophy which is which I wanted from the start. But the idea is to get it in the philosophy of science technology and culture. I want to be able to get my hands a little dirty and at the same time help advise policy makers on the ethics funding and decision-making related to the above. 

That way I can come by and all of my legal background with what I really love. I've built my own computers since I was a teenager and still do. I've learned all the Deep machine learning algorithms on hugging face and how to use them and the various different architectures and which ones work better for what as well as how to put side rails on there and fine-tune them and even develop agents. I've learned python some JavaScript some type scripts HTML and and learning some real-time operating system and BusyBox which is the current project. But one of my side ideas is to make add backpack generator for outdoor people does lightweight so engineering grade 3D printing or I was thinking about doing bioplastic with milk and graphing powder as I saw the strength and if you added a UV clear coat protection on the outside it would be very strong and you can make it quite durable especially if you add some ways for them to self-repair and self patch. And it could be carried and easily in the backpack and attached to a tripod to add to the flexible solar panels people are carrying for bad weather. 

I was thinking about a metal version made from cast home forged and using a reusable or a sand based casting where I use a bunch of positives and just make a bunch of the parts and then hand polish them and hand make the gears and outer casing. But then why the windings using your serpentine coil and the design with the very long slender serpentine design or perhaps the axial flux for two or three perhaps but using either engineering-grade and there's a new version of engineering grade which is cheaper and the guy who made it made it to democratize engineering-grade printing filament. he also makes crystalline fabric for new batteries and he's on YouTube here and they were talking about his filament it was like 3D nerd is the name of the channel maybe... But the filament apparently has the same strength as engineering grade polycarbonate Walnut crossing an enormous amount of money the same price more or less as PLA and the ability to use a normal home printer like a bamboo which he was using. 

Is the name of the company I think is called Z polymers or Z filaments the Man's last name is Zimmerman and he used to work for Bell Labs but his actual idea for this filament is to make engineering grade filament that's affordable for everyone these words were to democratize the strength and range of printing filaments. 

I'm not sure if you have to anneal the print afterwards it's possible but it can be easily printed by a normal 3D printer and maybe you have to fill the crystallize it afterwards in the oven but I have to re-watch the video.

author: @l0rd18
text:
Hi Robert,
Thank you so much for everything that you do, these projects are amazing!
I have a weekend project to try to build a small portable generator for camping/hiking and this looked perfect. The mechanical part of the project printed perfectly but when I get to the generator part I'm not able to get the same results as you.
I have wound a serpentine coil using 0.2 mm copper, about 200 turns, 300 doesn't fit in the case for me,  and 36 10x2 mm magnets. 
I don't have an oscilloscope to test it out properly, but on a  multi-meter I barely get 0.6 V and 0.8 mA.
Do you have a suggestion on what could be my problem? My magnets are not powerful enough? Or would the serpentine coil winding be the issue?

replies.author: @l0rd18
replies.text:
One of the issues was with my multi-meter, after switching to a new one the voltage is going up to 5V. It's not the 21V that you got in your test, but my wind source was a hair dryer that goes to a max of 2 m/s. The second would be that I don't have a 6mm rod so I printed one that does not fit perfectly and also causes the LHS/RHS to rub on the stator.
Thank you again for posting this video and all the details to it. It's very fun to work and play with these.

author: @AquackwithaKnack
text: I have been working on practically the exact same thing. However, my brief is to make a counter-rotating spherical drone. You beat me to posting a video about it! I've made a few prototypes and one scares me as I put a very powerful motor on it. I do get a fair amount of thrust out of it but haven't measured it to compare with a conventional prop yet. It doesn't feel like I get more but it may be more efficient at higher speeds than the motor can reach.

author: @glencahalin4786
text:
Nice :-) 
For me thinking about efficiency isn't perusing a "best figure on paper" claim, it's more thinking  things like "can we make this start generating at lower wind speeds than previous version does?" Essentially can we get more real World use/payback without adding too much extra to the cost, effort and maintenance of it. Which this looks like it does very nicely indeed.

author: @michaelmarino7216
text:
In the process of building one of these and wondering a couple of items. 
1) Making the blades aerodynamic to increase the life effect which should increase rpm's
2) Increasing the number of blades by one or two to decrease the gap and possibly increase lift even more
3) A slight increase in diameter to allow two rows of magnets, increasing the area of the wire being directly acted upon by the motion of the fields.

These are just thought that I am kicking around and will try to design myself and see what the results are. Will gladly share.

author: @zdenekbreza3770
text: I think a torus could be more effective than a sphere. The vanes (blades) forming the sphere still create a centrifugal force that increases friction, but if the vanes were pointing inward to form a torus, the centripetal force would create a more coherent flow and reduce friction.

author: @Reallyunreallyreal
text: Also if you link 3 dc motors in a tube with MIT's new props you can most likely run them in a series/parallel switching circuit and have it run continuously, by it's own.

author: @Reallyunreallyreal
text: I've done experiments with different type props run in series both on separate motors and also one with a double ended 12v motor (old 12v cig lighter oscillating fan) which is using a three blade plastic to pull a two blade metal. It works well. The props and motor are in 6" cardboard tube. Runs with other fans off a standard 12v adapter and a circuit to help it go.

author: @daveh6356
text: Nice optimisation Robert. I can see this design passing the not-too-ugly test for a rooftop, especially one with an existing mounting (Old satellite, TV antenna, vent, chimney). How large can you make these?

author: @kuqicouture4803
text:
Don't make it larger, make it modular.
Stack them 4 or 5 high in a frame.
Line frames up together to get desired 'swept area'.

Or, stack frames at 90 degrees to form a rectangular box or 60 degrees to form a trapezoidal post.

author: @Nicewoodworkingulove
text:
awesome. I also just watched the video about the making of the serpentine coil. whith the formula: Length serpentine = (π * D * N) + (π * D^2 / 4) + (π * H) for when the serpentine coil goes around a cylinder shaped coil. While in this omnidirectional wind turbine this is not the case. Could I still use the same formula? or should I just wrap a wire around this flat syle coil to mesure the distance without the use of a formula. 

Thanks for the great videos. I love this kind of projects.

author: @anthonybrayshaw8783
text: Excellent video as usual , I'm currently designing a sand battery power system that will run A/C ,heater ,fridge \freezer and hot and cold water to go inside a camper van, and this duel rotor set up gives me a fantastic idea of a power tunnel to power it all 👍

replies.author: @begin47
replies.text: I also have a camper van whose storage battery is a Tesla module; solar on roof with alt. charging when underway. I have long been intrigued by the possibility of using wind while underway to charge. How is your work going if you don’t mind sharing? Thanks!

replies.author: @austindavies6371
replies.text: @begin47 Very nice idea with the wind charging and very little drag thanks to the size.

replies.author: @anthonybrayshaw8783
replies.text: @begin47 hi , well I'm a member of van life UK on face book IV put up a circuit diagram for how the sand battery circuit will work currently gathering all the components to put that together , the wind generator is a really simple design tbh that I actually got from hydroponic growing tbh 🤣 the idea it lots of small generators that spin really fast , and the tube length could be altered according to van length , I think an ideal design would-be 2 tubes one down each side of the van leaving the middle for storage or a small solar and storage as the power needed to stay off grid would-be reduce due to the sand battery picking up the slack ,

replies.author: @begin47
replies.text:
Thanks for getting back to me. Nice to meet you. I don’t even know what sand battery is. I’ll have to do some research on that. I have been following the evolution of wind generators with the goal of finding something of high efficiency and small size that would work to recharge my Tesla storage battery. 
I have 1 fan, 5 solar panels and a swamp cooler on the roof, so not much room up there. Are you using wind a generator(s) at this point and if so, what type and size?

author: @matteonicoli
text: massive, cool, madness, game changer! Many thanks!  I understand it's omnidirectional, but was the base fixed? And does the wind direction matter a lot in yield?

author: @jamjar20049
text:
This should be on the roofline of every home, factory, office, etc in the country harvesting wind energy wherever it is fitted. However, making it available freely on Tinkercad surely means that a patent won't be granted? To scale up production would need investment, and how many times have we seen on Dragons Den and the like a great product being demonstrated and the question comes " do you have a patent for this?", "Er no" is the answer followed swiftly by " I'm out". 
Relying on hobbyists producing them on an ad hoc basis will result in some being made mainly for self consumption. I know Robert's drive is to encourage others to do their own research which is fair enough.I feel that this should and could be scaled up for the mass market, meaning a production line in a factory or two, an inward investment would be required to set that up with government encouragement to enable the generators to be fitted for the consumer. A ready market and lower carbon emissions, a win win situation.

author: @R2NOTU

replies.author: @R2NOTU
replies.text: @nexxus0320 by reducing the lock up areas you'll need a second wheel with small magnets  lined up to reduce drag it works on generators 21/2 times out input the Japanese have appliances with it in them . Your magnets need to be adjustable.

author: @jamest.5001
text: I wonder if this could be made much larger. Using steamed strips of bamboo. Glued together with liquid nails, or epoxy of something ,and possibly wrap with fiberglass to protect it. Making a roughly 96" ball, on a tower of 2" heavy wall tubing, maybe form a tripod. From 1.5" tubing braced evry 10' with 1/2" tubing. And 1/8" cables near the top, with a 2" pipe being the final 10' being 50' tall total,  maybe have it tilt on two legs  to about 45° angle , and the top tilt to another 45° making ig easy to install and service.  The whole thing should be 100-150lbs at most , this being tgf reason for thd cable bracing.  Use a liniar actuator to tilt the top  or maybe a cable and hinge. Uding a 1/4"cable to keep the top pole latched straight, and to tilt down when the tower is tilted,  build a 48" ring  as the rotor and stator,  form a 2"x4" magnet using two steel bars, and a couple magnets , rough 1.5"x 1/8" n52, or close,  use 3 staters and 2 rotors,   using serpent coils on each starter,   epoxy the magnets together and to the rotor,  place poly foam between the magnets, then wrap with fiberglass, Wrap the coils around the foam  blocks and wrap them with fiberglass also, only make it possible to cut the fiberglass and remove it like a shell from the coils, re wrap the coils and glue it back on in the future!  Just a rough guess it should do 2-3kw! with afairly strong Wind. 20mph maybe 1kw or so,

author: @WesternIronworks
text: I think those would do great in ducts but i wonder if there would be a better setup that already exists. I am also wondering if there needs to be more of a gap between the blades of the front and the back,like you have mentioned on the bigger double rotor that there needs to be a gap between them. maybe make them taller by using a funnel at the base of each one so the channel the air into the center hole better and to create more gap between the blades. just some thoughts that I am not sure are worth the effort when there might be something better already for that compact size.
